task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 15 Escalation media step out of specification

abstract.p: This topic describes the how to act when the media step is out of specification.

section.title: Description
section.p: Because the media step is out of specification, more detailed information is required that enables Customer Support LFG to analyze the situation.
An escalation must contain the following 5 steps:

li.p: Be sure that task [C] is correctly executed.
Run SDS test 15-6-022 S&S measure media steps in production print mode [Special Tests]
Create a new trace log via the On site procedure 2 with Settings Editor. See : How to get the trace files

note.p: Back office procedure 1 via ORS will not contain the data and can therefore not been used.

li.p: Send a Media Step Out Of Specification escalation email with subject:
C1650#MediaStepOOS#<SerialNumber> towards lfg.support@cpp.canon containing the trace data as attachment. (approximately 200MB)

note.p: Do not use normal email since the trace data is too large.

li.p: TIP: Use e.g. wetransfer containing the trace data.
Advantage of this is that you will:
- receive a notification that the package is sent
- receive a notification when the package is being downloaded.
Escalate to your local NSO specialist with the request to escalate to headquarters using the regular escalation procedure.
The escalation should mention "Media Step Out Of Specification escalation" containing at least the serial number and the email notification that the tracedata is being send to lfg.support@cpp.canon.
